---
title: "Gen3: Public BTS Replication"
subtitle: "Nationwide Public Data | 19,150,000 flights | Reproducible Results"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: full
    smooth-scroll: true
---

```{=html}
<nav style="margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <small><a href="index.html">Home</a> › <a href="research-overview-en.html">Research Overview</a> › Gen3</small>
  <br>
  <a href="research-overview-en.html" style="display:inline-block;margin-top:0.5rem;padding:0.3rem 0.8rem;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:4px;text-decoration:none;font-size:0.9rem;">← Back to Research Overview</a>
</nav>
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Overview

**Motivation:** Gen2 used AA's proprietary Snowflake data, which cannot be publicly reproduced. Validation on public data was needed.

**Data Source:** BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) public data

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Scale:** 19,150,000 U.S. domestic flights (2021-2023)
:::

**Temporal split:**

| Set | Period | Rows |
|---|---|---|
| Train | 2021-2022 | ~12.4M |
| Val | 2023 H1 | ~3.3M |
| Test | 2023 H2 | ~3.5M |

**Key Differences:**

- Gen2: Single airline (AA), single hub (DFW), 804K flights
- Gen3: All U.S. carriers, all airports, 19.15M flights

**Two versions:**

| Version | Features | HPO | Test AUC |
|---|---|---|---|
| V2 Full Pipeline | 41 (with tail chain + congestion) | Optuna 100-trial | **0.7681** |
| V4 Clean Baseline | 34 (no tail chain, no congestion) | Default | **0.7011** |

## V2 Full Pipeline {#v2}

**Content:** Full replication of Gen2 methodology - SHAP feature selection + Optuna HPO + XGBoost

**41 Features (7 source groups):**

| Source Group | # Features | SHAP % |
|---|---|---|
| Tail Chain | 6 | 35.5% |
| Schedule (BTS) | 6 | 18.6% |
| Congestion | 3 | 14.4% |
| Origin METAR | 8 | 9.4% |
| Airport (FAA) | 3 | 7.5% |
| TAF Forecasts | 9 | 7.4% |
| Dest METAR | 6 | 7.1% |

**Model comparison (Test set):**

| Model | AUC |
|---|---|
| XGBoost (Optuna) | **0.7681** |
| CatBoost | 0.7627 |
| Random Forest | 0.7497 |

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Best: XGBoost AUC 0.7681** (Optuna HPO, lr=0.0145, depth=8)
:::

**Top-5 SHAP features:** `dep_hour` (Schedule) > `turnaround_pressure` (Tail Chain) > `prev_2_flight_delay` (Tail Chain) > `turnaround_min` (Tail Chain) > `prev_flight_arvl_delay` (Tail Chain)

## V4 Clean Baseline {#v4}

**Core purpose:** Establish the strictest leakage-free baseline - removing all features requiring aircraft identity or aggregation.

### 12-Rule Leakage Control Framework

| # | Rule | V4 Compliance |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | T_pred = CRS_DEP_TIME - 2h | PASS |
| 2 | Temporal gating: all features <= T_pred | PASS |
| 3 | Aircraft identity: no TAIL_NUM, no tail chain | PASS |
| 4 | Actual outcomes: DEP_TIME/ARR_TIME removed | PASS |
| 5 | Aggregation: no congestion (baseline) | PASS |
| 6 | Weather timestamps: METAR backward, TAF issue < T_pred | PASS |
| 7 | Network propagation: not used | PASS |
| 8 | Label leakage: ARR_DELAY -> binary, then dropped | PASS |
| 9 | Dataset split: temporal only | PASS |
| 10 | Global statistics: not used | PASS |
| 11 | Feature verification: per-feature audit log | PASS |
| 12 | Leakage audit: assertion pass required | PASS |

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**34/34 features passed leakage audit**
:::

**34 SAFE Features (6 source groups):**

| Source Group | # Features | SHAP % |
|---|---|---|
| Schedule (BTS) | 8 | **59.4%** |
| Origin METAR | 8 | 11.7% |
| Airport Static (FAA) | 4 | 8.6% |
| Dest METAR | 6 | 8.0% |
| TAF Forecasts | 7 | 6.8% |
| Calendar | 1 | 5.4% |

**Model comparison (Default hyperparameters):**

| Model | AUC |
|---|---|
| CatBoost | **0.7011** |
| XGBoost Default | 0.7009 |
| Random Forest | 0.6915 |

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Best: CatBoost AUC 0.7011** (default hyperparameters, 34 features)
:::

### V2 vs V4 Difference

V4 is **0.0670 AUC** lower than V2, quantifying the combined contribution of:

1. Tail chain features (6 features)
2. Congestion features (3 features)
3. Optuna HPO
4. SHAP feature selection

## Aeolus Benchmark {#aeolus}

**Purpose:** Compare GBDT and Deep Learning models on the same BTS data.

::: {.callout-tip}
## GBDT Comprehensively Outperforms DL

**XGBoost AUC = 0.687 >> Best DL AUC = 0.623** (ΔAUC = +0.064)
:::

All 4 GBDT models outperformed all 7 DL models, consistent with Gen2 Phase 2 findings.

## SHAP Analysis {#shap}

### V2 SHAP (with tail chain)

`dep_hour` is the single most important feature. Tail Chain accounts for 35.5% overall, the largest source group.

| Source Group | SHAP % |
|---|---|
| Tail Chain | 35.5% |
| Schedule | 18.6% |
| Congestion | 14.4% |
| Origin METAR | 9.4% |
| TAF | 7.4% |
| Airport | 7.5% |
| Dest METAR | 7.1% |

### V4 SHAP (without tail chain)

After removing tail chain and congestion, Schedule's SHAP share jumped from 18.6% to **59.4%**.

| Source Group | SHAP % |
|---|---|
| Schedule | **59.4%** |
| Origin METAR | 11.7% |
| Airport Static | 8.6% |
| Dest METAR | 8.0% |
| TAF | 6.8% |
| Calendar | 5.4% |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Key Insight

Regardless of whether tail chain is included, **Schedule is always the most important source group**. In V2, tail chain suppressed schedule's apparent share, but V4 reveals schedule's true dominance.
:::

## Schedule Dominance {#schedule}

::: {.callout-tip}
## Core Finding: The Schedule Tells You If a Flight Will Be Delayed

In V4 Clean Baseline, schedule-derived features account for **59.4%** of total SHAP. Most of the predictable component of delays is already determined the moment the schedule is published.
:::

**Plain Language:** Delays are not random accidents but systematic structural problems. Departure time, aircraft type, route traffic - these factors, already determined when the schedule is published, dominate delay predictability.

**Consistency across versions:**

- V2 (with tail chain): Schedule 18.6%, but `dep_hour` is still #1 individual feature
- V4 (without tail chain): Schedule **59.4%**, structural dominance is even more pronounced

**Comparison with Literature:**

- FDPP-ML (2023, *Journal of Big Data*): Stated "no current study can predict delays from schedule features alone" and then did so themselves
- But no paper has used SHAP to quantify and explain "what proportion does schedule dominate, and why"
- Rigorously validated on 19.15M flights nationwide, not a geographic anomaly

## Weather Contribution {#weather}

::: {.callout-warning}
## Correction: Weather Is Not "Near-Zero"

V4 Clean Baseline SHAP results: Origin METAR 11.7% + Dest METAR 8.0% + TAF 6.8% = **total 26.5%**
:::

**Weather contribution difference between V2 and V4:**

| Version | Weather Total | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| V2 | ~23.9% | Suppressed by tail chain (35.5%), lower apparent share |
| V4 | **26.5%** | No tail chain, weather's true contribution is clearer |

**Corrected conclusions:**

- Weather still accounts for **26.5%** under strict leakage control, not "near-zero"
- Gen2 V9's TAF bringing +0.002 AUC also confirms weather has a positive contribution
- The previous "weather near-zero" claim was likely an artifact of weather being suppressed by tail chain in V2

**Comparison with Literature:**

- Hatipoglu & Tosun (2024, MDPI): Found weather unimportant, attributed to Turkey's stable climate
- Alfarhood et al. (2024, Wiley): Weather unimportant in Saudi Arabia, attributed to desert climate
- **Our finding**: Under strict leakage control, weather still accounts for about one-quarter of SHAP contribution

::: {.callout-tip}
## Implication for Literature

Most existing literature's weather feature importance may be overestimated due to leakage. However, under strict conditions, weather remains a meaningful predictor (~26.5%), just far below schedule's dominance.
:::

:::

```{=html}
<script>
window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  var hash = window.location.hash.replace('#', '');
  if (!hash) return;
  var tabs = document.querySelectorAll('[data-bs-toggle="tab"], [data-bs-toggle="pill"]');
  for (var i = 0; i < tabs.length; i++) {
    var target = tabs[i].getAttribute('data-bs-target') || tabs[i].getAttribute('href') || '';
    if (target.indexOf(hash) !== -1) {
      tabs[i].click();
      break;
    }
  }
});
</script>
```
